rationales.hedonism: He deliberately turns his phone off, cooks, reads, and savors an unproductive afternoon because his body needs pleasure and rest.
He tries to enjoy music and wine, but abandons the evening and returns to work because he cannot stay present in the relaxing thing he wanted.
rationales.achievement: His attention keeps returning to whether he finished enough, and he ultimately opens the laptop to keep pushing the work forward.
rationales.benevolence: Part of what pulls him back to work is the belief that someone on his team might be stuck if he does not step in.

rationales.hedonism: After a ten-hour day, he denies himself the bath and the hour off he wants, showing clear sacrifice of comfort and recovery.
rationales.achievement: He chooses to prep for the next day's review instead of resting, prioritizing work performance over immediate relief.

rationales.hedonism: He takes an unstructured coffee break without his laptop, lingers in a quiet pleasant moment, and explicitly recognizes that he wants more of this slower kind of day.
Even at a restaurant he had wanted to try, he cannot actually enjoy the meal because work keeps colonizing the experience.
